---
title: "Student Lab Guide"
subtitle: "DE4W Capstone Project"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
---

# Goal

In this lab, you will work through an end-to-end wearable machine learning pipeline:

```text
raw accelerometer data
→ windows
→ features
→ model
→ evaluation
→ API
→ dashboard
→ live phyphox prediction
```

# Setup

Install dependencies:

```bash
uv sync
```

`uv` is used so the whole class works with the same Python environment and dependency versions.

Check imports:

```bash
uv run python -c "import app.api, app.training, app.evaluation, app.model_utils, app.pipeline; print('final imports work')"
```

# Data Placement

Place WISDM phone accelerometer files in `data/`.

Expected names:

```text
data_1600_accel_phone.txt
data_1601_accel_phone.txt
...
```

The generated feature table is:

```text
data/features_df.csv
```

# Render The Course Book

Use the project Python environment:

```bash
PATH=.venv/bin:$PATH quarto render docs
```

If plain `quarto render docs` fails because Jupyter or PyYAML is missing, use the command above.

# Train Or Export The Model

The project already includes model artifacts.

If you need to regenerate them, work through the machine learning and evaluation chapters.

Expected artifacts:

```text
models/activity_model_random_forest.joblib
models/feature_columns.joblib
models/activity_labels.joblib
models/model_metadata.json
```

# Start FastAPI

Run:

```bash
uv run uvicorn app.api:app --reload
```

`uvicorn` is the local web server that makes the FastAPI prediction app reachable in your browser.

Keep this terminal open while you test the API.

Open:

```text
http://127.0.0.1:8000/health
```

You should see `model_loaded: true`.

You can also open Swagger UI:

```text
http://127.0.0.1:8000/docs
```

Swagger UI lets you inspect `GET /health` and `POST /predict` in the browser.

Use `/health` first.

It checks whether the model artifacts can be loaded.

# Start Streamlit

Open a second terminal and run:

```bash
uv run streamlit run dashboard.py
```

Streamlit creates the live demo dashboard directly from Python code.

The dashboard asks for:

- phyphox URL
- window size
- demo duration
- Live phyphox or Replay Mode

Keep FastAPI running in the first terminal if your demo uses the API service.

Use Replay Mode if phyphox or the network is unavailable. It still demonstrates
the Sensor Stream, Window, Feature Vector, Model and Prediction flow.

# Connect phyphox

On your phone:

1. Open phyphox.
2. Start an acceleration experiment.
3. Enable remote access.
4. Copy the shown URL.
5. Paste it into Streamlit.

Before starting the Streamlit demo, test the phyphox URL in your laptop browser.

If the browser cannot open the URL, Streamlit cannot read the phone either.

::: {.callout-warning}
## Hotspot Warning

University networks such as eduroam often block direct phone-to-laptop communication.

If the phyphox URL does not open on the laptop, use a smartphone hotspot, local router, or travel router.
:::

# Troubleshooting

## phyphox URL Does Not Open

Make sure phone and laptop are on the same network.

eduroam may block direct communication.

Try:

- smartphone hotspot
- local WiFi router
- travel router

## API Health Fails

Check that model artifacts exist in `models/`.

Expected files:

```text
models/activity_model_random_forest.joblib
models/feature_columns.joblib
models/activity_labels.joblib
models/model_metadata.json
```

## Prediction Fails With Missing Features

The model expects all 20 feature columns saved in `models/feature_columns.joblib`.

Do not manually invent feature names.

Use the feature extraction pipeline.

## Streamlit Stops With A phyphox Error

Read the dashboard message.

Most failures are caused by:

- wrong phyphox URL
- remote access disabled
- phone asleep
- phone and laptop on different networks
- eduroam blocking local traffic

## Predictions Are Slow To Appear

The dashboard needs a full Window before it can predict.

A five-second Window means the first Prediction appears only after enough samples have been collected.

Use a smaller Window for a faster but potentially noisier demo.

# Completion Checklist

You are done when you can:

- render the Quarto book,
- explain random split vs subject-wise split,
- start `/health`,
- start Streamlit,
- describe the live prediction pipeline,
- explain the eduroam/hotspot lesson.
